# UC-22.13.24 · Unauthorized Physical Access Attempts at PSP

## Description

We aggregate denied, forced, and tailgate physical access events for rapid triage and evidence of physical perimeter monitoring.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes physical and sourcetypes pacs:access, pacs:access`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Map readers to PSP inventory. (2) Push high severity to SOC bridge. (3) Optional join to VMS syslog. (4) Weekly trending for reliability. Indexes required: `index=physical`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pacs:access`, `sourcetype=pacs:access``; Lookups: `cip_psp_register`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=physical` `sourcetype=pacs:access`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map readers to PSP inventory. (2) Push high severity to SOC bridge. (3) Optional join to VMS syslog. (4) Weekly trending for reliability.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=physical sourcetype=pacs:access earliest=-24h
    (result="denied" OR event_type IN ("forced","tailgate","passback"))
| lookup cip_psp_register reader_id OUTPUT psp_name bes_site
| stats count by event_type, psp_name, bes_site
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Unauthorized Physical Access Attempts at PSP (CIP-006-6 R2)** — We aggregate denied, forced, and tailgate physical access events for rapid triage and evidence of physical perimeter monitoring.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=physical` `sourcetype=pacs:access`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: physical; **sourcetype**: pacs:access. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=physical, sourcetype=pacs:access, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by event_type, psp_name, bes_site** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT historian (PI, Proficy) or the vendor HMI/operations console the operator trusts. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar (event types), Table (raw), Single value (tailgates)

## SPL

```spl
index=physical sourcetype=pacs:access earliest=-24h
    (result="denied" OR event_type IN ("forced","tailgate","passback"))
| lookup cip_psp_register reader_id OUTPUT psp_name bes_site
| stats count by event_type, psp_name, bes_site
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Stacked bar (event types), Table (raw), Single value (tailgates). Key fields: `bes_site`, `event_type`, `psp_name`.
